name: Comptroller of Public Accounts
notes: Texas has no income tax; Comptroller handles sales tax, property tax oversight, and state spending. Publishes state expenditure data online.

name: Department of Family and Protective Services
notes: Child protective services, adult protective services, and childcare licensing. Individual case records largely exempt but aggregate data available.

name: Department of Public Safety
notes: State troopers, Texas Rangers, driver license records, and criminal history. Crash reports available through CR-3 system.

name: Department of Transportation
notes: Construction contracts, traffic crash data, and highway project records. District offices handle district-level requests.

name: Health and Human Services Commission
notes: Umbrella agency for health, human services, Medicaid. Nursing home inspections, CPS data (redacted), and benefits administration records.

name: Office of the Attorney General
notes: Also serves as arbiter of open records disputes — agencies must seek AG ruling before withholding. The Open Records Division issues letter rulings.

name: Office of the Governor
notes: Governor's Office processes requests through general counsel. May request AG ruling to withhold records.

name: Public Utility Commission of Texas
notes: Regulates electric and telecom utilities. Rate filings and docket records available through interchange system. Post-Winter Storm Uri, grid reliability records heavily requested.

name: Railroad Commission of Texas
notes: Despite the name, primarily regulates oil, gas, and pipeline operations. Well production data, pipeline safety records, and injection well permits. Much data in online GIS system.

name: Secretary of State
notes: Election administration, business filings, and notary records. Voter registration data available in bulk.

name: State Auditor's Office
notes: Audit reports, state employee classification data, and performance reviews of state agencies. Most audit reports published online.

name: Texas Commission on Environmental Quality
notes: Air/water quality permits, enforcement actions, and contamination site data. Central registry searchable online.

name: Texas Department of Criminal Justice
notes: Execution records, death row data, use-of-force reports, and inmate death records commonly requested. Execution drug sourcing is frequently litigated.

name: Texas Education Agency
notes: School district accountability data, STAAR testing results, and school safety reports. Much aggregate data on TEA website.

name: Texas Legislature / Legislative Council
notes: Legislature is subject to TPIA for administrative records. Bill files, fiscal notes, and committee records generally available. Internal deliberative records often withheld.

name: Texas Parks and Wildlife Department
notes: Game warden incident reports, park usage data, and wildlife management records.

name: Texas Workforce Commission
notes: Unemployment claims data, wage and hour complaints, and workforce development records.
